name: Container Glass
description:
Resource cycle for soda-lime container glass.

Layout: sketch (hand SVG). Switch to ai_drawn in the UI to restore the earlier circle layout.
Per-edge `flow` sets arrow thickness (relative material amount).
co2e_basis: Order-of-magnitude allocations from container-glass LCAs (cradle-to-gate often ~1.0–1.3 kg CO₂e/kg low-cullet; FEVE ~0.67 t CO₂ saved per t remelted cullet). Not a certified EPD. Node totals are illustrative — do not double-count overlapping paths when comparing scenarios.

nodes.label: Harvest
nodes.summary: Quarry sand, limestone & dolomite; produce soda ash; blend batch
nodes.details:
Batch is typically ~70% silica sand, ~15% soda ash, plus limestone/dolomite.

1. Mine and wash silica sand; crush limestone and dolomite.
2. Produce or buy synthetic soda ash (largest upstream GHG in the batch).
3. Dry, weigh, blend; truck batch to the glass plant.

Furnace process CO₂ from carbonates is counted under Manufacture.
nodes.co2e_note: ~0.20 kg CO₂e/kg. Mostly synthetic soda ash (~0.10–0.15) plus mining, drying, short-haul transport.

nodes.label: Manufacture
nodes.details:
1. Melt batch in a regenerative or oxy-fuel furnace (~1400–1600 °C).
2. Form on IS machines (blow/press).
3. Anneal in a lehr; inspect and pack empty ware.

Cullet from the Recycle loop rejoins the melt (folded into Recycle → Fill as remelted ware ready to fill). High cullet cuts fuel and process CO₂ (~3% energy / ~5% CO₂ per 10% cullet).
nodes.co2e_note: ~0.85 kg CO₂e/kg low-cullet: ~0.55–0.65 fuel + ~0.15–0.25 process CO₂ + ~0.05–0.10 forming/annealing. High cullet can cut this toward ~0.4–0.5.

nodes.label: Recycle
nodes.summary: Collect, sort, treat cullet → remelt → rejoin Fill
nodes.details:
Bottle banks / kerbside → colour sort → cullet plant → furnace remelt → empty ware back into Fill.

Credit assumes cullet actually displaces virgin batch melt.
nodes.co2e_note: Net ≈ −0.63 kg CO₂e/kg: ~+0.04 collect/sort/treat minus ~−0.67 avoided virgin production (FEVE-scale) when cullet actually remelts.

nodes.label: Reuse
nodes.summary: Return, wash, inspect — then back into Fill
nodes.details:
Deposit–return reuse: reverse logistics → industrial wash (hot water, caustic) → inspection → rejoin Fill.

Each wash costs energy but avoids remelting. Trip rate and regional loops dominate the climate outcome.
nodes.co2e_note: ~0.06 kg CO₂e/kg per industrial wash cycle. One successful reuse avoids ~1+ kg virgin remanufacture when it displaces a new bottle.

nodes.label: Fill
nodes.summary: Rinse, fill, cap, and label at the bottling plant
nodes.details:
Empty glass (virgin from Manufacture via Deliver, remelted from Recycle, or washed from Reuse) is rinsed/sterilised, filled, capped, labelled, and packed.

Inbound empty ware and outbound filled freight are Deliver edges, not a node.

Product contents’ footprint is out of scope for the glass functional unit.
nodes.co2e_note: ~0.04 kg CO₂e/kg glass for rinse/fill/cap line utilities attributed to the container (not the beverage). Varies widely with pasteurisation and line speed.

nodes.label: Use
nodes.summary: Consumer or venue uses the product; bottle becomes empty again
nodes.details:
Use-phase GHG for the glass itself is negligible. The important choice is the next edge:

• Landfill — material lost
• Recycle — remelt / rejoin at Fill
• Reuse — wash and rejoin at Fill
nodes.co2e_note: ~0 kg CO₂e for the glass object during use. Climate impact is decided by which outgoing branch is taken.

nodes.label: Landfill
nodes.details: Residual-waste collection and burial. Glass is inert in landfill (little methane), but embodied carbon and material are lost permanently.
nodes.co2e_note: ~0.02 kg CO₂e/kg collection + landfill ops. The large climate cost is forgoing Recycle/Reuse credits, not landfill gas.

edges.co2e_note: ~0.03 kg CO₂e/kg for a typical regional empty-ware haul (~150–300 km).
~0.05 kg CO₂e/kg for a representative filled outbound chain (~200–400 km total).
